questions.text: Create a 5-slide presentation deck (PowerPoint, Google Slides, or PDF) that proposes a unique blockchain application built on MST. Your presentation must cover the following slides:
questions.explanation:
Slide 1 (Problem): 4 marks. 2 marks for a clear, specific problem statement. 2 marks for linking the problem to failures discussed in Modules 6.1 - 6.3.
Slide 2 (MST Solution): 5 marks. 1 mark per correctly identified pillar (minimum three). 2 marks for explaining how the pillars work together in your specific use case.
Slide 3 (Why Other Chains Fail): 4 marks. 2 marks per chain (1 for identifying the limitation, 1 for linking it to Sub-Module 6.2 content).
Slide 4 (User Journey): 4 marks. 2 marks for a clear, realistic user flow. 2 marks for explicitly addressing at least one user nightmare from Sub-Module 6.3 and showing how MST resolves it.
Slide 5 (Business Model & Impact): 3 marks. 1 mark for a plausible business model. 1 mark for a cost comparison (MST vs another chain). 1 mark for quantifying social or economic impact.
questions.modelAnswer:
📊 Marks Breakdown

Slide 1 (Problem): 4 marks. 2 marks for a clear, specific problem statement. 2 marks for linking the problem to failures discussed in Modules 6.1 - 6.3.

Slide 2 (MST Solution): 5 marks. 1 mark per correctly identified pillar (minimum three). 2 marks for explaining how the pillars work together in your specific use case.

Slide 3 (Why Other Chains Fail): 4 marks. 2 marks per chain (1 for identifying the limitation, 1 for linking it to Sub-Module 6.2 content).

Slide 4 (User Journey): 4 marks. 2 marks for a clear, realistic user flow. 2 marks for explicitly addressing at least one user nightmare from Sub-Module 6.3 and showing how MST resolves it.

Slide 5 (Business Model & Impact): 3 marks. 1 mark for a plausible business model. 1 mark for a cost comparison (MST vs another chain). 1 mark for quantifying social or economic impact.

Submission: Upload your 5-slide deck as a PDF or provide a shareable link. Record a 5-minute video walkthrough (optional for bonus feedback, not graded). Total: 20 marks.
